title: Heap Allocation Contracts and Failure Handling · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working allocation wrapper with explicit outcomes while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of allocation wrapper with explicit outcomes while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply malloc and calloc size, initialization, alignment, zero-size, and failure contracts without dereferencing assumptions.
Propagate allocation failure without partial-state leaks, null dereferences, or impossible-error assumptions.
Choose and test a project policy for zero elements rather than depending on an implementation-specific returned pointer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Malloc Calloc Contracts
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 9113. Produce bounded C evidence to handle allocation failure and zero-size policy explicitly; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
